item.title: Editors of economics journal resign after association renews Taylor & Francis contract
item.description: Forty-one editors, associate editors and advisory board members resigned from their roles at the Review of Social Economy after the Association for Social Economics, which owns the journal, renewed…

item.title: Exclusive: A couple paid more than $800,000 for a gene-editing therapy for their daughter. She died, and it wasn’t made public
item.description: Illustration: Daria Lada An investigation by Science and Retraction Watch has uncovered details about a clinical trial that resulted in the death of its sole patient: a 6-year-old girl with a rare …

item.title: Journal retracts breast cancer paper 10 years after sleuth flagged issues
item.description: A biomedical journal has retracted a paper by a cancer researcher who sued her former institution over misconduct allegations. The action comes a decade after a sleuth first raised concerns about t…

item.title: Exclusive: Medical student in Nepal behind busy research factory
item.description: A second-year medical student in Nepal is at the center of an international research network churning out everything from systematic reviews and meta-analyses to case reports and database studies, …

item.title: Publisher investigating peer reviewer for alleged bribery scheme
item.description: Deagreez/iStock Earlier this year, Tushar Sen, an independent researcher based in New Delhi, submitted a manuscript to Security and Privacy about the modeling of polymorphic malware. On May 15, a p…

item.title: Weekend reads: Private info in ‘most’ arXiv preprints; why did George Church decline authorship; 28 misconduct cases in China
item.description: If your week flew by — we know ours did — catch up here with what you might have missed. The week at Retraction Watch featured: ‘Disappointed’: Cochrane journal asked researchers to publish article…

item.title: Harvard cancer researchers earn retraction for image duplication
item.description: A group of researchers at Brigham and Women’s Hospital and Harvard Medical School in Boston have lost a paper for image duplication following an investigation by the two institutions. The pap…

item.title: Now, 240: More than a decade later, journals are still retracting Joachim Boldt’s papers
item.description: Martin Tramèr was editor-in-chief of the European Journal of Anaesthesiology during what he once called in print “The Boldt debacle.” The misconduct scandal involving hundreds of papers by Joachim …

item.title: Sage retracts eight papers by former Radboud ‘rising star’ for compromised peer-review process
item.description: The publisher Sage has retracted eight papers by a former “rising star” from a Dutch university for a “compromised” peer-review process at a journal he edited. Last week, Re…

item.title: ORI sanction and news coverage prompts sleuthing, retraction
item.description: Four months after the Office of Research Integrity sanctioned a former postdoc for falsifying images in grant reports, a Science journal is retracting a paper by the researcher for image duplicatio…
